title: Weekly Essay Writing Schedule | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Weekly Writing Practice by Language Level
description: Plan your weekly writing essays and word count by CEFR proficiency level. FSI-based recommendations for language learners. Free, instant calculator.
intro: Learning a language requires hours of deliberate, spaced practice. According to the FSI, the time investment varies by language family: romance languages like Italian and Portuguese require ~600 hours, Germanic languages like German ~900 hours, and East Asian languages like Mandarin and Japanese ~2200 hours. The strategy that works: consistency beats intensity. This calculator estimates your weekly essay writing practice based on your target proficiency level. Data updated for 2026 with periodic reviews to keep recommendations current.
keyTakeaway: Formula: Weekly writing practice. Direct calculation based on your language proficiency level.
useCases: ESL and language learners planning weekly writing schedules
Teachers designing writing assignments for different proficiency levels
Polyglots tracking practice across multiple languages
Test prep for Cambridge, TOEFL, or IELTS exams
Quick reference for consistent daily practice
explanation:
**Learning a language** requires sustained effort over hundreds of hours. Cognitive science (spacing effect, Ebbinghaus), immersion, and active practice are proven pillars supported by FSI and Cambridge research.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator applies writing frequency guidelines based on your target proficiency level, recommending essay count and word length. It validates inputs against typical ranges and alerts you to unusual values.

## Important Notes

These are guideline recommendations. For critical decisions (exam prep, certification), consult language professionals or official course standards. Data updated April 2026 with periodic reviews.

faq.q: How many essays should I write per week at B1?
faq.a: At B1 (Intermediate), aim for 1-2 essays weekly, around 150 words each. Focus on clarity and basic organization.

faq.q: What's the recommended writing frequency for B2?
faq.a: B2 (Upper-Intermediate) requires 2-3 essays per week, approximately 200 words each. Add nuanced vocabulary and varied sentence structures.

faq.q: How often should I write at C1 level?
faq.a: C1 (Advanced) calls for 3 essays weekly, 350-500 words per essay. Focus on complex argumentation and sophisticated expression.

faq.q: What does C2 writing practice look like?
faq.a: C2 (Mastery) requires 3+ essays weekly, 500+ words with sophisticated argumentation and native-like expression.

faq.q: Where can I get my essays corrected?
faq.a: Try iTalki, LangCorrect, Preply, or hire a private tutor. Many platforms offer affordable feedback on your writing.
Yes. All Hacé Cuentas calculators are completely free with no registration required.
These are FSI and Cambridge-based guidelines suitable for most learners. For official exam requirements, verify with the exam board.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We never collect or transmit your data to any server.
